name: B2B Partnerships Promo
summary: A spreadsheet row becomes a finished, personalized asset set for a partner agent — flyer, messaging image and email — generated and filed without anyone opening a design file.
objectives: Produce one personalized asset set per partner agent per campaign, without anyone rebuilding a file by hand.
Keep one partner roster as the single source of truth, so a campaign is built from it rather than re-typed into it.
Let partner agents register and edit their own details, instead of routing every change through one person.
Serve every market the partnership runs in, in its own language.

outcomes.text: A spreadsheet row becomes a finished asset set — a co-branded PDF flyer, a messaging image and an HTML email — filed automatically into the right folder for its region, campaign and agent. The assets for shipped campaigns come out of this pipeline.
Partner agents register and edit themselves through a web form, and the roster they populate is what campaigns are built from. Adding a campaign no longer means re-entering the people in it.

The form is hardened against the failures that actually cost data: concurrent edits are locked, submissions are validated on the server rather than trusted from the browser, and user input is rendered as text instead of markup.

Nothing is published by hand. The email is built and passed through a blocking quality check before release, and the spreadsheet's own scripts are deployed from version control rather than pasted into a console.
It runs on live campaigns, not in rehearsal — the registration form has been used against the real roster, and the fixes since then came from watching people use it rather than from a plan.

milestones.title: Establish one spreadsheet schema and one placeholder contract for every asset
Make the partner roster the source that campaigns are built from
Ship the self-service registration form for partner agents
Harden the form against concurrent edits, bad input and data loss
Deploy the spreadsheet's scripts from version control instead of by hand
Unify on one email pipeline, built and checked before anything is published
Run it on live campaigns and fix what real use exposed
Decide whether the registration form needs its own Spanish, Portuguese and Turkish versions, now that the team is using it
